questions.stem:
—______ you ever ______ to the Shenzhen Science Museum? 
—Yes, I went there last month. It was amazing!
questions.explanation: 正确答案为B。本题考查现在完成时与一般过去时的区别。句中有'ever'（曾经），这是现在完成时的标志性时间状语，表示过去经历对现在的影响。结构为'Have/Has + 过去分词'。A选项是一般过去时，虽可回答具体时间，但问句中有ever时应用现在完成时；C是一般现在时；D是现在进行时表将来。技巧口诀：'ever/never/already/yet'，完成时态记心间。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The book ______ I bought yesterday is about environmental protection. It is very interesting.
questions.explanation: 正确答案为C。本题考查定语从句关系词的选择。先行词'the book'指物，且在从句中作宾语（I bought the book），因此用which或that引导。A选项who用于指人，在从句中作主语；B选项whom用于指人，在从句中作宾语；D选项whose表示'……的'，在从句中作定语。解题技巧：先行词是物选which/that，是人选who/whom，表所属用whose。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The new teaching building ______ by the workers and it will be put into use next term.
questions.explanation: 正确答案为C。本题考查现在完成时的被动语态。根据后半句'will be put into use next term'（下学期将投入使用）可知，教学楼已经被建好，强调过去动作对现在的影响（已经完工，即将使用），应用现在完成时的被动语态'has been built'。A是主动语态一般现在时；B是主动语态现在进行时；D是一般过去时被动语态，仅表示过去被建，未体现对现在的影响。技巧：看时间标志词和语境暗示，强调'已完成'用现在完成时。
questions.label: 语法题

options: A. where is the nearest bookstore
B. where the nearest bookstore is
C. where does the nearest bookstore
D. where the nearest bookstore was
questions.explanation: 正确答案为B。本题考查宾语从句的语序。宾语从句必须使用陈述句语序（主语+谓语），不能用疑问句语序。A和C都是疑问句语序（is/does在主语前），首先排除。D选项时态错误，主句'Could you tell me'中的could是委婉语气，不是过去时，从句陈述客观事实应用一般现在时is。技巧口诀：宾语从句用陈述，主句时态定从句，客观真理永一现。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The ______ of the mountain is covered with snow all year round. You can see the white peak from far away.
questions.explanation: 正确答案为B。本题考查名词词义辨析。根据后句'You can see the white peak'（你能看到白色的山峰）和'covered with snow'（被雪覆盖）可知，此处指'山顶'。A选项foot表示'山脚'；C选项side表示'侧面'；D选项bottom表示'底部'。解题技巧：利用上下文语境线索（peak提示top），联系地理常识（山顶积雪）。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: We should ______ our best to reduce plastic waste and protect the ocean.
questions.explanation: 正确答案为A。本题考查固定搭配'try one's best'（尽某人最大努力）。B选项make常搭配make progress（取得进步）、make a decision（做决定）；C选项do常搭配do homework（做作业）、do exercise（做运动）；D选项take常搭配take action（采取行动）、take care of（照顾）。技巧：积累常用动词短语，注意介词搭配，'try one's best to do sth'是中考高频考点。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: The story sounds ______. Nobody believes it is true.
questions.explanation: 正确答案为B。本题考查词性转换及前缀用法。根据后句'Nobody believes it'（没人相信）可知，故事'难以置信'。un-是否定前缀，unbelievable意为'难以置信的'。A选项believable（可信的）与句意相反；C选项believe是动词原形；D选项belief是名词（信仰）。技巧：掌握常见否定前缀un-/dis-/im-，根据上下文逻辑（转折、因果）判断词义方向。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started an environmental protection club. I decided to join it because I really care about the environment. Every Friday afternoon, we meet in the classroom to discuss how to make our school greener.

Last week, we (8)______ a special activity. We collected waste paper and plastic bottles in the school. At first, some students thought it was (9)______ and didn't want to help. But when we explained the importance of recycling, they changed their minds. By the end of the day, we (10)______ over 50 kilograms of waste. Our teacher was very proud (11)______ us. She said our small actions could make a big (12)______ to the world. I felt happy because I knew we were doing something meaningful for our planet.
questions.explanation: 正确答案为B。本题考查动词词义辨析及语境理解。根据宾语'a special activity'（一次特别活动）可知，应选'organized'（组织）。A选项had（有）搭配不够具体；C选项found（发现）与语境不符，活动是主动策划而非发现；D选项lost（丢失）与下文描述的积极活动相悖。技巧：完形填空注意动词与宾语的搭配合理性，以及上下文情感色彩（积极/消极）。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started an environmental protection club. I decided to join it because I really care about the environment. Every Friday afternoon, we meet in the classroom to discuss how to make our school greener.

Last week, we (8)______ a special activity. We collected waste paper and plastic bottles in the school. At first, some students thought it was (9)______ and didn't want to help. But when we explained the importance of recycling, they changed their minds. By the end of the day, we (10)______ over 50 kilograms of waste. Our teacher was very proud (11)______ us. She said our small actions could make a big (12)______ to the world. I felt happy because I knew we were doing something meaningful for our planet.
questions.explanation: 正确答案为A。本题考查形容词情感色彩及逻辑关系。根据后文'But... they changed their minds'（但他们改变了想法）和'didn't want to help'（不想帮忙）可知，起初他们认为活动'boring'（无聊的）。B、C、D都是积极词汇，与'didn't want to help'矛盾。技巧：关注转折词But/However，前后语义通常相反，利用逻辑关系反推答案。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started an environmental protection club. I decided to join it because I really care about the environment. Every Friday afternoon, we meet in the classroom to discuss how to make our school greener.

Last week, we (8)______ a special activity. We collected waste paper and plastic bottles in the school. At first, some students thought it was (9)______ and didn't want to help. But when we explained the importance of recycling, they changed their minds. By the end of the day, we (10)______ over 50 kilograms of waste. Our teacher was very proud (11)______ us. She said our small actions could make a big (12)______ to the world. I felt happy because I knew we were doing something meaningful for our planet.
questions.explanation: 正确答案为A。本题考查原词复现及语境理解。第二段首句明确提到'We collected waste paper...'，此处为原词复现，表示'收集了50多公斤垃圾'。B选项bought（购买）与收集废品语境不符；C选项sold（售卖）应是收集后的步骤，而非当天活动；D选项threw（扔）与环保主题相悖。技巧：完形填空注意'原词复现'和'同义词复现'，前文出现的词后文可能再次出现。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started an environmental protection club. I decided to join it because I really care about the environment. Every Friday afternoon, we meet in the classroom to discuss how to make our school greener.

Last week, we (8)______ a special activity. We collected waste paper and plastic bottles in the school. At first, some students thought it was (9)______ and didn't want to help. But when we explained the importance of recycling, they changed their minds. By the end of the day, we (10)______ over 50 kilograms of waste. Our teacher was very proud (11)______ us. She said our small actions could make a big (12)______ to the world. I felt happy because I knew we were doing something meaningful for our planet.
questions.explanation: 正确答案为C。本题考查固定搭配'be proud of'（为……感到骄傲）。这是中考必考短语。A选项in常搭配be proud in（无此搭配）；B选项with常搭配be pleased with（对……满意）；D选项at常搭配be good at（擅长）。技巧：积累常用形容词短语，特别是proud/pleased/good/interested等词与介词的固定搭配。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started an environmental protection club. I decided to join it because I really care about the environment. Every Friday afternoon, we meet in the classroom to discuss how to make our school greener.

Last week, we (8)______ a special activity. We collected waste paper and plastic bottles in the school. At first, some students thought it was (9)______ and didn't want to help. But when we explained the importance of recycling, they changed their minds. By the end of the day, we (10)______ over 50 kilograms of waste. Our teacher was very proud (11)______ us. She said our small actions could make a big (12)______ to the world. I felt happy because I knew we were doing something meaningful for our planet.
questions.explanation: 正确答案为C。本题考查固定搭配'make a difference'（产生影响/发挥作用）。句意：她说我们的小行动可以给世界带来大影响。A选项make a decision（做决定）；B选项make a mistake（犯错误）；D选项make a plan（制定计划），均与语境不符。技巧：注意名词与动词的搭配，make a difference是环保类文章高频短语。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
In today's digital age, more and more teenagers spend long hours staring at screens. Whether it's for online classes, homework, or entertainment, screen time has become a major part of daily life. However, doctors warn that excessive screen time can seriously harm our eyes.

Dr. Li Ming, a famous eye doctor in Shenzhen, suggests the "20-20-20" rule to protect our eyes. It means that for every 20 minutes of screen time, we should look at something 20 feet away (about 6 meters) for at least 20 seconds. This simple habit helps relax the eye muscles and reduce tiredness.

Besides following the 20-20-20 rule, there are other ways to keep our eyes healthy. First, make sure the screen is at least 50-70 cm away from your eyes. Second, blink more often to keep your eyes wet. Third, get enough sleep and eat more green vegetables and carrots, which are rich in vitamins good for eyesight.

Many students in our school have tried these methods. Wang Wei, a 14-year-old boy, said, "I used to have dry eyes and headaches after using the computer. But after following the 20-20-20 rule for a month, I feel much better now."

Remember, our eyes are the windows to the world. Taking good care of them today will help us see a clearer tomorrow.
questions.stem: According to the passage, how long should we look at something 20 feet away according to the "20-20-20" rule?
questions.explanation: 正确答案为B。本题考查细节理解。根据第二段第二句'we should look at something 20 feet away (about 6 meters) for at least 20 seconds'（我们应该看20英尺外的物体至少20秒）可知，正确答案是20秒。A、C、D选项的时间与原文不符。技巧：细节题采用'关键词定位法'，在题干中找'20 feet away'和'how long'，回原文定位到第二段即可找到答案'20 seconds'。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解

questions.group_passage:
In today's digital age, more and more teenagers spend long hours staring at screens. Whether it's for online classes, homework, or entertainment, screen time has become a major part of daily life. However, doctors warn that excessive screen time can seriously harm our eyes.

Dr. Li Ming, a famous eye doctor in Shenzhen, suggests the "20-20-20" rule to protect our eyes. It means that for every 20 minutes of screen time, we should look at something 20 feet away (about 6 meters) for at least 20 seconds. This simple habit helps relax the eye muscles and reduce tiredness.

Besides following the 20-20-20 rule, there are other ways to keep our eyes healthy. First, make sure the screen is at least 50-70 cm away from your eyes. Second, blink more often to keep your eyes wet. Third, get enough sleep and eat more green vegetables and carrots, which are rich in vitamins good for eyesight.

Many students in our school have tried these methods. Wang Wei, a 14-year-old boy, said, "I used to have dry eyes and headaches after using the computer. But after following the 20-20-20 rule for a month, I feel much better now."

Remember, our eyes are the windows to the world. Taking good care of them today will help us see a clearer tomorrow.
questions.stem: The underlined word "excessive" in Paragraph 1 probably means ______.
questions.explanation: 正确答案为B。本题考查词义猜测。根据上下文'warn that... can seriously harm our eyes'（警告说……会严重伤害眼睛）以及常识，只有'过多的'屏幕时间才会伤害眼睛。A选项proper（适当的）、C选项limited（有限的）、D选项necessary（必要的）均不会与'harm'搭配。技巧：利用上下文逻辑关系（因果、转折）和常识猜测词义，ex-前缀常表示'向外、超过'，excessive即'过度的'。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测

questions.group_passage:
In today's digital age, more and more teenagers spend long hours staring at screens. Whether it's for online classes, homework, or entertainment, screen time has become a major part of daily life. However, doctors warn that excessive screen time can seriously harm our eyes.

Dr. Li Ming, a famous eye doctor in Shenzhen, suggests the "20-20-20" rule to protect our eyes. It means that for every 20 minutes of screen time, we should look at something 20 feet away (about 6 meters) for at least 20 seconds. This simple habit helps relax the eye muscles and reduce tiredness.

Besides following the 20-20-20 rule, there are other ways to keep our eyes healthy. First, make sure the screen is at least 50-70 cm away from your eyes. Second, blink more often to keep your eyes wet. Third, get enough sleep and eat more green vegetables and carrots, which are rich in vitamins good for eyesight.

Many students in our school have tried these methods. Wang Wei, a 14-year-old boy, said, "I used to have dry eyes and headaches after using the computer. But after following the 20-20-20 rule for a month, I feel much better now."

Remember, our eyes are the windows to the world. Taking good care of them today will help us see a clearer tomorrow.
questions.stem: What can we infer from Wang Wei's words in Paragraph 4?
options: A. He doesn't like using computers at all.
B. He wants to see an eye doctor immediately.
C. The 20-20-20 rule is helpful for eye health.
D. Dry eyes cannot be cured by any method.
questions.explanation: 正确答案为C。本题考查推理判断。Wang Wei说：'我过去用电脑后眼睛干涩头痛，但遵循20-20-20规则一个月后感觉好多了'，可推断出该规则对眼睛健康有帮助。A选项'一点也不喜欢用电脑'过度推断，他仍使用电脑只是学会了保护；B选项'想立即看医生'与原文不符，他已经好转；D选项'干眼症无法治愈'与原文'feel much better'矛盾。技巧：推理题要基于文本信息，避免过度推断或主观臆断，注意转折词But后的内容常是重点。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断

questions.group_passage:
In today's digital age, more and more teenagers spend long hours staring at screens. Whether it's for online classes, homework, or entertainment, screen time has become a major part of daily life. However, doctors warn that excessive screen time can seriously harm our eyes.

Dr. Li Ming, a famous eye doctor in Shenzhen, suggests the "20-20-20" rule to protect our eyes. It means that for every 20 minutes of screen time, we should look at something 20 feet away (about 6 meters) for at least 20 seconds. This simple habit helps relax the eye muscles and reduce tiredness.

Besides following the 20-20-20 rule, there are other ways to keep our eyes healthy. First, make sure the screen is at least 50-70 cm away from your eyes. Second, blink more often to keep your eyes wet. Third, get enough sleep and eat more green vegetables and carrots, which are rich in vitamins good for eyesight.

Many students in our school have tried these methods. Wang Wei, a 14-year-old boy, said, "I used to have dry eyes and headaches after using the computer. But after following the 20-20-20 rule for a month, I feel much better now."

Remember, our eyes are the windows to the world. Taking good care of them today will help us see a clearer tomorrow.
questions.stem: What is the best title for the passage?
options: B. How to Protect Our Eyes in the Digital Age
C. The Life of Dr. Li Ming
questions.explanation: 正确答案为B。本题考查主旨大意/最佳标题。全文围绕数字时代（digital age）如何保护眼睛展开，介绍了20-20-20规则及其他护眼方法。A选项'眼镜的历史'只在文中未提及；C选项'李医生的生活'只是文中细节，非主旨；D选项'为什么我们需要电脑'偏离主题，文中强调的是用电脑时的护眼而非需要电脑。技巧：最佳标题应概括全文核心内容，通常包含主题词（eyes/eye protection）和范围（digital age/screen time），避免以偏概全或扩大范围。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意

questions.group_passage:
Waste sorting has become an important part of our daily life in Shenzhen. It helps protect the environment and save resources. (17)______. For example, kitchen waste can be turned into fertilizer, while paper and plastic can be recycled into new products.

(18)______. First, we should learn the four categories: recyclable waste, harmful waste, kitchen waste, and other waste. Each type goes into a different colored bin. (19)______. Second, we need to clean the containers before throwing them away. This makes recycling easier and more efficient.

(20)______. Many communities have volunteers who stand by the bins to help residents sort their waste correctly. With everyone's effort, our city will become cleaner and greener.
options: A. However, it is not always easy to sort waste correctly at the beginning.
B. Then, we can take some simple steps to sort waste properly.
C. As a result, waste sorting has been widely promoted across the city.
D. Different types of waste are processed in different ways.
E. In addition, the government has set up special programs to help people learn about waste sorting.
questions.explanation: 正确答案为D。本题考查段落衔接和逻辑关系。空后'For example'（例如）提示前一句是总述，后文举例说明：厨余垃圾变成肥料，纸和塑料回收成新产品。D选项'Different types of waste are processed in different ways'（不同类型的垃圾以不同方式处理）正好概括后文例子。A表转折，后文无转折含义；B引出步骤，但后文是举例而非步骤；C表结果，此处无因果关系；E补充政府项目，与后文例子无关。技巧：关注连接词For example/For instance，其前通常是总述句。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
Waste sorting has become an important part of our daily life in Shenzhen. It helps protect the environment and save resources. (17)______. For example, kitchen waste can be turned into fertilizer, while paper and plastic can be recycled into new products.

(18)______. First, we should learn the four categories: recyclable waste, harmful waste, kitchen waste, and other waste. Each type goes into a different colored bin. (19)______. Second, we need to clean the containers before throwing them away. This makes recycling easier and more efficient.

(20)______. Many communities have volunteers who stand by the bins to help residents sort their waste correctly. With everyone's effort, our city will become cleaner and greener.
options: A. However, it is not always easy to sort waste correctly at the beginning.
B. Then, we can take some simple steps to sort waste properly.
C. As a result, waste sorting has been widely promoted across the city.
D. Different types of waste are processed in different ways.
E. In addition, the government has set up special programs to help people learn about waste sorting.
questions.explanation: 正确答案为B。本题考查段落主题句。空后出现'First... Second...'，提示这是步骤说明。B选项'Then, we can take some simple steps to sort waste properly'（然后，我们可以采取一些简单步骤正确分类垃圾）作为主题句引出下文步骤。A表转折，与后文步骤无关；C表结果，位置不当；D是17题答案；E补充说明，不适合引出步骤。技巧：看到First/Second/Third等序数词，前文通常有'take steps/methods/ways'等总述词。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
Waste sorting has become an important part of our daily life in Shenzhen. It helps protect the environment and save resources. (17)______. For example, kitchen waste can be turned into fertilizer, while paper and plastic can be recycled into new products.

(18)______. First, we should learn the four categories: recyclable waste, harmful waste, kitchen waste, and other waste. Each type goes into a different colored bin. (19)______. Second, we need to clean the containers before throwing them away. This makes recycling easier and more efficient.

(20)______. Many communities have volunteers who stand by the bins to help residents sort their waste correctly. With everyone's effort, our city will become cleaner and greener.
options: A. However, it is not always easy to sort waste correctly at the beginning.
B. Then, we can take some simple steps to sort waste properly.
C. As a result, waste sorting has been widely promoted across the city.
D. Different types of waste are processed in different ways.
E. In addition, the government has set up special programs to help people learn about waste sorting.
questions.explanation: 正确答案为A。本题考查逻辑转折关系。前文讲'First, we should learn...'（首先我们应该学习……），后文讲'Second, we need to...'（其次我们需要……），中间插入A选项'However, it is not always easy...'（然而，起初正确分类并不总是容易的），形成'建议-困难-建议'的逻辑，使文章更有层次。B已用于18题；C表结果，此处无因果；D已用于17题；E与上下文衔接不畅。技巧：However表转折，用于引出问题或困难，使文章内容更丰富，避免平铺直叙。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
Waste sorting has become an important part of our daily life in Shenzhen. It helps protect the environment and save resources. (17)______. For example, kitchen waste can be turned into fertilizer, while paper and plastic can be recycled into new products.

(18)______. First, we should learn the four categories: recyclable waste, harmful waste, kitchen waste, and other waste. Each type goes into a different colored bin. (19)______. Second, we need to clean the containers before throwing them away. This makes recycling easier and more efficient.

(20)______. Many communities have volunteers who stand by the bins to help residents sort their waste correctly. With everyone's effort, our city will become cleaner and greener.
options: A. However, it is not always easy to sort waste correctly at the beginning.
B. Then, we can take some simple steps to sort waste properly.
C. As a result, waste sorting has been widely promoted across the city.
D. Different types of waste are processed in different ways.
E. In addition, the government has set up special programs to help people learn about waste sorting.
questions.explanation: 正确答案为E。本题考查段落衔接和补充说明。最后一段提到'Many communities have volunteers...'（许多社区有志愿者……），E选项'In addition, the government has set up special programs...'（此外，政府设立了特别项目……）中的'In addition'（此外）表示补充，与后文'volunteers'形成'政府-社区'的并列支持体系。A已用于19题；B已用于18题；C表结果，通常用于结尾总结，但后文还有'With everyone's effort...'；D已用于17题。技巧：In addition/Furthermore/Moreover用于补充同类信息，看到后文有新措施时，前文可能是'政府/学校/社区'等不同主体的支持。
questions.label: 短文填空
